title: The Legends of Rocks, Roger, and Oden Surface
description: On pages 7–10, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6, Luffy, Unknown person #7, Roger, Unknown person #8 as The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
scene: The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Another is that many of these incidents have been wiped from the pages of history by the WG.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: How- Ever...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: their enormity reached everyone's ears at the time.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: The fateful clash happened 38 years ago,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: On God Valley.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The day that the news that the strongest crew in the world, the rocks pirates

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Was heard round the world!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: The inexorable Advance of Evil, nearly unstoppable,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: had been stopped by a marine vice Admiral by the name of GARP! GARP's name soon spread the world over,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and he became known as the "hero of the Marines."

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: So that was the beginning!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There's already so many legends of GARP's heroic deeds, I didn't know which to believe...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: If you ask the man himself, however,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Remember someone

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You'll find him rather tight-lipped on the subject.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Huh? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: He'll give you the historical facts, sure,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: But not much more.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Big mom and Kaido?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: One after another...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: The first reason

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: never made it into the papers, but...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: GARP had to fight alongside a pirate in that battle.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: A pirate?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: The other is...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: He fought to protect celestial dragons.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: WHA-?! where did the celestial dragons come from?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And I thought protecting the celestial dragons is the duty of the Marines!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Garp's moral code doesn't permit him to fulfill that duty.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Is because becoming one will place him squarely under the thumb of the celestial dragons.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Despite doing so much, the fact that he cannot shake this story is proof, if nothing, of what an achievement it is and the degree of popularity he has enjoyed.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: At God Valley, in order to protect celestial dragons and their slaves,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: garp and Roger, who both happened to meet there, joined forces

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and piped out the rocks pirates! That is the full story of the "God Valley incident."

dialogue.speaker: Roger
dialogue.text: He fought alongside the pirate King?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What a Cast! celestial dragons, Roger, and Rocks?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What in the world happened on that island! I've never even heard of a place called God Valley!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The location of the island of God Valley

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: is no longer depicted on any map.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: And, in reality, the entire island itself

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: has vanished without a trace.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: An island whose existence the world government desired very much to keep a secret

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: has vanished.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Still interested in asking questions about it?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Once upon a time, a buccaneer by the name of rocks sought to become the pirate king.

summary: This section advances the Wano - Oden and the Old Era storyline with 45 dialogue beats. The story turns to Oden’s past, showing how his life, Whitebeard, Roger, and Orochi shaped Wano’s tragedy.
